core_behavior: The system must send a notification to the user when their order is shipped.
This notification will inform the user that their order has been dispatched and provide them with relevant shipping details.
inputs: Order ID: a unique identifier for the order
User ID: a unique identifier for the user who placed the order
Shipping details: information about the shipping carrier, tracking number, and estimated delivery date
User contact information: the user's email address and/or phone number, depending on their preferred notification method
outputs: A notification message sent to the user via their preferred method (email and/or SMS)
A record of the notification being sent, including the timestamp and notification method used
assumptions: The system will use a pre-existing notification service to send emails and SMS messages.
The system will assume that the user's contact information is up-to-date and valid.
The system will use a template-based approach to generate the notification message, with placeholders for dynamic data such as the order ID and shipping details.
The system will send notifications in real-time, as soon as the order is marked as shipped in the system.
failure_modes: Notification service failure: if the notification service is unavailable or returns an error, the system will retry sending the notification up to 3 times before logging an error and notifying the system administrator.
Invalid user contact information: if the user's contact information is invalid or outdated, the system will log an error and notify the system administrator.
Order data inconsistency: if the order data is inconsistent or missing required information, the system will log an error and notify the system administrator.
edge_cases: Order cancellation: if an order is cancelled after the notification has been sent, the system will send a follow-up notification to the user informing them of the cancellation.

Order update: if an order is updated after the notification has been sent (e.g. shipping carrier or tracking number changes), the system will send a follow-up notification to the user with the updated information.
User notification preferences: if a user has opted out of receiving notifications or has specified a preferred notification method, the system will respect their preferences.
security_constraints: Data encryption: all user contact information and order data must be encrypted in transit and at rest.
Access control: only authorized system administrators and users with the necessary permissions will be able to access and modify order data and user contact information.
Audit logging: all system activities, including notification sending and errors, must be logged and auditable.
performance_constraints: Notification latency: notifications must be sent within 1 minute of the order being marked as shipped.
Throughput: the system must be able to handle a minimum of 100 notifications per minute.
Error rate: the system must maintain an error rate of less than 1% for notification sending.
